data.title: 'Fix Your AI': Elon Musk Rips Into Anthropic after $380B Valuation
data.description:
AI startup Anthropic raised $30 billion in Series G funding, giving the company a post-money valuation of $380 billion. Despite the funding milestone, Elon Musk sharply criticized Anthropic's AI, telling the company to "Fix it." Musk, who runs the rival AI firm xAI, posted on X that Claude AI "hates Whites & Asians, especially Chinese, heterosexuals, and men," calling it "misanthropic and evil."

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Musk has previously also pointed out the irony in Anthropic's name, saying the human-focused AI company has ended up producing technology he calls "misanthropic."

Anthropic's New Funding Round

Anthropic's latest Series G funding round was led by GIC and Coatue. The round was co-led by D. E. Shaw Ventures, Dragoneer, Founders Fund, ICONIQ, and MGX.

In its official announcement, Anthropic said the funding will help boost its research, product development, and infrastructure, reinforcing its role as a leader in enterprise AI and coding. The company also noted that in less than three years since earning its first dollar in revenue, its run-rate revenue has already reached $14 billion, growing more than tenfold each year.

Musk vs. Anthropic

Anthropic is best known for its Claude large language models and competes directly with xAI's chatbot Grok. Musk has been critical of Anthropic before, with reports suggesting the startup cut off xAI's access to Claude models.

This latest confrontation also highlights rising tensions in the AI industry, where companies race to develop powerful systems while facing scrutiny over bias and safety. In this competitive landscape, success increasingly depends not only on technological breakthroughs but also on public battles among industry leaders.

Interestingly, Anthropic's rapid valuation growth reflects strong investor appetite for leading AI developers. By contrast, despite Musk's push, market data shows xAI remains a smaller player globally, with generative AI traffic still dominated by OpenAI's ChatGPT and Google's (GOOGL) Gemini.

Which Is the Best AI Stock, According to Analysts?

Most of the leading AI companies are still private, which limits direct investing options. Still, investors can gain exposure through publicly traded players tied to the AI ecosystem. Using TipRanks' Stock Comparison tool, investors can compare top AI-related stocks and dig deeper into analyst ratings, price targets, and key insights. Below is a screenshot for reference.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Intel Stock (NASDAQ:INTC) Notches Up Despite Losses to AMD
data.description:
Chip stock Intel (INTC) has been fighting hard to get back lost market share, but some new reports say it may have quite a ways to go before it gets there. Word from Mercury Research says that one of Intel's biggest rivals, AMD (AMD) took a lot of Intel's market share, and is not eager to give it up. But despite this, Intel still managed to gain ground of its own, with a modest gain in share prices in Friday afternoon's trading.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The recent supply strictures Intel has been operating under are proving fertile ground for AMD, reports note, with AMD now accounting for just over a third, 36%, of CPUs going into desktop systems. This time last year, AMD accounted for just over a quarter of that market at 27%. AMD's gains in laptops, meanwhile, were a bit more subdued, as AMD represented 26% of laptops against the 23.8% it held the same time the previous year.

Intel is still the clear leader in laptops, though; it accounts for 74% of the laptop PC market by itself. Given earlier reports that the laptop market is a much busier market for Intel anyway, it may have decided to concede parts of the desktop market to focus on the laptop market instead. Intel has a clear lead in the server market as well, holding 71% against AMD's 28.8%.

A Growing Need for Chip Research

Intel has been frantically trying to make a comeback from its losses over the years, but one thing that seems to go with little fanfare is chip research. Certainly, Intel has made some moves in this vein recently, as it did with the ZAM prototype. We have heard about the Nova Lake lineup and the chips to follow. But beyond that generation, where is Intel on pure research?

Increasingly, eyes in the government are looking to Intel to step in and fill in some of the gaps caused by what some call an over-reliance on domestic chip production. And Intel is certainly working in that vein; reports suggest it has "thousands" of scientists working in its Hillsboro labs. But some question if, perhaps, this is actually enough. Overseas labs are doing their own research, and in some ways, it may be ahead of even Intel.

Is Intel a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on INTC stock based on eight Buys, 21 Holds and four Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 96.95% rally in its share price over the past year, the average INTC price target of $48.21 per share implies 2.66% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: UBS Warns AI Could Trigger a Wave of Corporate Loan Defaults
data.description:
The stock market has already punished many software companies that appear vulnerable to artificial intelligence, but investment firm UBS (UBS) believes that the pressure may soon spread to credit markets. In a recent note, UBS head of credit strategy Matthew Mish warned that AI disruption could trigger a wave of corporate loan defaults. He said that tens of billions of dollars in debt, especially among private equity-owned software and data services firms, could default over the next year as these businesses face growing competitive pressure from AI.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Speaking to CNBC, Mish explained that markets are beginning to factor in what he called a "rapid, aggressive disruption scenario." According to Mish, investors had previously assumed that any meaningful AI disruption would not happen until 2027 or 2028. However, recent advancements have forced many to reassess that timeline. As a result, sentiment shifted from viewing AI as mostly positive for tech companies to viewing it as a winner-take-all dynamic, where leaders such as Anthropic and OpenAI could seriously threaten incumbents.

Interestingly, in UBS' base case, leveraged loan and private credit markets, which together total about $3.5 trillion, could see $75 billion to $120 billion in new defaults by late 2026 as default rates rise. At the same time, Mish outlined a more severe "tail risk" scenario in which defaults could reach roughly double those baseline estimates. In that case, he warned of a potential credit crunch, where funding becomes harder to access, and leveraged credit markets see a repricing. Still, UBS is not yet predicting that outcome, noting that much depends on how quickly companies adopt AI.

Is IGV Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on the iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (IGV) stock based on 91 Buys, 22 Holds, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average IGV price target of $136.49 per share implies 64.3% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: "The Moon is Our Proving Ground": Boeing Stock (NYSE:BA) Gains as it Takes Aim at Space Travel
data.description:
Admittedly, aerospace giant Boeing (BA) might not be the biggest name in space travel right now. After the debacle that was Starliner, it is probably easier to think Boeing is the punchline to a joke about space rather than a major figure in space travel. But Boeing is eager to get back up there, and is working accordingly. Investors seemed pleased enough, based on the fractional gain in share prices seen in Friday afternoon's trading.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Boeing actually has a hand in the Artemis II launch, which was delayed from its original date back to sometime after March 6. Boeing, part of Space Launch System, is part of the 212-foot core stage of the rocket going up. Program manager Natasha Wiest calls the core stage "…the backbone of the rocket." And Wiest also looks for Boeing to gain some significant credibility as a result of the Artemis II launch.

"The moon is our proving ground, where we learn to live and work in space far from Earth before going even farther," Wiest noted. And this was indeed an important note for Wiest to deliver, given the sheer number of space-related setbacks Boeing has seen over the last year or so. The Artemis II's launch was delayed. Starliner has been relegated to hauling cargo. But Boeing is not giving up on space, and Wiest's optimism is likely welcome.

Indian Boost

Closer to Earth, India proved a welcome source of support for Boeing once more, as India looked to spend big money—about $40 billion worth—on augmenting its armed forces. The air force will be getting a fresh supply of Rafale jets, and Boeing will also have a hand in the restocking as India buys a set of P-8I reconnaissance aircraft.

India, looking around at its unsettled neighbors like Pakistan and China, has decided to modernize its military accordingly, and Boeing's P-8I is a major part of that effort. The Rafale jets, meanwhile, are also part of a move to shore up fighter strength, as several of India's fighters are reaching end-of-service times, including the Jaguar, the Mirage 2000, and at least two classes of MiG.

Is Boeing a Good Stock to Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on BA stock based on 14 Buys, one Hold and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 29.79% rally in its share price over the past year, the average BA price target of $269.50 per share implies 11.22% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: 10-Year Treasury Yield Slides to 2-Month Low as Inflation Drifts Down
data.description:
The 10-year Treasury yield is down by 4 bps to 4.064% on Friday, marking the lowest level since December 2025.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

A lower-than-expected Consumer Price Index (CPI) reading for January contributed to the decline. Inflation rose by 2.4%, below the estimate of 2.5% and marking the lowest rate of growth since May 2025. Easing inflation strengthens the case for interest rate cuts, increasing demand for bonds and pushing yields lower.

Why Is the 10-Year Treasury Yield Down Today?

While the Fed doesn't directly control long-term yields, cooler inflation lowers expectations for future interest rates and reduces inflation risk, making Treasuries more attractive to investors.

The central bank is scheduled to meet for its next Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) on March 18. The odds of a 25 bps rate cut at the meeting are slim, with the CME FedWatch tool pricing in a 9.8% chance. However, those odds increase to 26.4% at the next meeting on April 29.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Dow Jones Today: DJIA Rallies on Cooling Inflation as Pentagon Withdraws Updated 1260H List
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is trading higher on Friday following an encouraging inflation reading from the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS).

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The Consumer Price Index (CPI) for January rose by 0.2% month-over-month, below the consensus estimate of 0.3%. It increased by 2.4% on an annual basis, below the expectation of 2.5% and registering the slowest pace of growth since May 2025. Core CPI, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, increased by 0.3% from the prior month and 2.5% annually, both of which were in line with their estimates.

"Headline CPI inflation was a touch softer than expected in January, delivering a welcome surprise to the downside at the beginning of the year," wrote Oxford Economics lead economist Bernard Yaros in a note.

This morning, the Pentagon published a document detailing the inclusion of Alibaba (BABA), Baidu (BIDU), Nio (NIO), and several other companies to the Section 1260H list. The list flags Chinese companies the Pentagon views as having ties to the country's military. However, the document has since been taken down from the Pentagon's website, with some attributing the move to the U.S. seeking to ease tensions before the Trump-Xi summit in April.

Elsewhere, the Financial Times reported that the Trump administration is planning to exempt some steel and aluminum products from tariffs in light of affordability concerns, which have affected products like drink cans and pie tins. Since the tariffs were imposed last June, the price of aluminum has risen by 24%, while steel has edged higher by about 4%. However, White House trade adviser Peter Navarro later denied the report, saying that Trump will maintain a "no exemptions, no exclusions" approach.

Earlier this week, a study from the New York Fed found that Americans absorb 90% of the increased costs from tariffs. The average tariff rate on U.S. imports has risen to 13% from 2.6%, with importers passing on higher costs to consumers.

The Dow Jones is up by 0.50% at the time of writing.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Fear Robinhood's Post-Earnings Slump? Analysts Say 'Frustrating' But Stay Bullish
data.description:
Robinhood Markets (HOOD), the popular retail trading platform, saw its stock plunge nearly 17% over the past two days after mixed Q4 results on February 10. Earnings per share (EPS) of $0.66 beat analysts' consensus estimate of $0.63 per share, but sales of $1.28 billion missed the $1.35 billion consensus forecast. The shortfall mainly came from a 38% drop in crypto transaction revenue to $221 million, driven by lower trading volumes.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

HOOD shares are down 37% year-to-date. The key question: Will this downward spiral continue, or are downside risks easing? Analysts offer a mixed but mostly optimistic view.

Analysts Trim Targets But Stay Bullish

Truist Financial analyst David Smith cut his price target from $130 to $120, still implying 68.7% upside potential, but kept his Buy rating. He called the Q4 report "frustrating," stating that outside of prediction markets, Robinhood had few positives. Key numbers like user growth and net deposits were weak in December alongside crypto weakness, while January was a bit better. He remains optimistic about Robinhood's long-term potential, despite short-term volatility.

Similarly, Needham analyst John Todaro slashed his price target to $100 (from $135), suggesting 40.6% upside, while keeping his Buy view. He noted that Q4 results were strong and met his estimates. Prediction markets particularly stood out, with January volumes hitting a record $3.5 billion in contracts. However, crypto volumes and take rates weakened in fiscal 26, along with options and equities. He expects crypto weakness to linger for two quarters before recovering.

Additionally, Piper Sandler analyst Patrick Moley kept his Buy rating but trimmed his price target from $155 to $135, implying 89.8% upside. Moley sees short-term headwinds but keeps his long-term view intact. "If you can stomach the volatility, HOOD is the best way to play secular growth in retail trading and the closest FinTech platform we've ever seen to achieving 'super app' status," Moley argued.

Key Takeaway

Analysts' concerns mostly center around short-term crypto and trading volume volatility, which make up a big chunk of Robinhood's revenue (crypto alone was about 17% of Q4 total). Yet, all three analysts see strong long-term potential in Robinhood's low-cost brokerage, expanding products like prediction markets, and retail trading growth.

Is HOOD a Long-Term Investment?

On TipRanks, HOOD stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 14 Buys and two Hold ratings. The average Robinhood Markets price target of $135.46 implies 90.5% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Waymo Pays DoorDash Drivers to Close Robotaxi Doors
data.description:
Waymo's robotaxis drive without human operators, but they still cannot handle every situation on their own. For instance, if a passenger gets out and leaves the door open, the car will not move until someone physically closes it. To address that issue, Waymo, which is owned by Alphabet (GOOGL), has launched a pilot program in Atlanta that pays gig workers to shut those doors. DoorDash drivers in the area receive notifications when a nearby Waymo has an open door so they can close it and allow the vehicle to continue operating.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The program drew attention after a Reddit post showed a DoorDash driver being offered $11.25 for completing the task. In response, Waymo and DoorDash (DASH) told CNBC that they are always looking for flexible ways for delivery drivers to earn extra income. Waymo also noted that future versions of its vehicles are expected to include automatic door-closing technology, though the company did not provide a timeline.

Meanwhile, Waymo is also working with Honk, an independent roadside assistance service, to pay users for similar help. According to The Washington Post, Honk users in Los Angeles have been offered up to $24 to close robotaxi doors. Although closing a door may seem like a small detail, it highlights a larger point. Even some of the most advanced autonomous systems still depend on human assistance for basic tasks. Even so, the company continues to expand and recently rolled out its next-generation robotaxi.

Are Google Shares a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on Alphabet stock based on 26 Buys and seven Holds assigned in the past three months. Furthermore, the average Alphabet price target of $377.23 per share implies 22.7% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Two Years to Unemployment: Microsoft Stock (NASDAQ:MSFT) Notches Up With New Projection of AI in White Collar Work
data.description:
Tech giant Microsoft (MSFT) just started one doozy of a countdown clock for anyone who works in an office setting, as it projects that artificial intelligence (AI) will automate most white-collar work, from lawyers to accountants and beyond, in somewhere around 12 to 18 months. But that was not the only prediction Microsoft brought out about AI. While Microsoft is regarding one of the greatest sea changes in the economy since the arrival of the internet as a thing of the very near future, investors were still reasonably pleased. Microsoft shares ticked up fractionally in Friday afternoon's trading.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Microsoft's Mustafa Suleyman noted that AI is reaching a point where "…most professional white-collar tasks" could be automated in the next two years, as AI has reached "…human-level performance" of most of its roles. This is actually being seen at Microsoft itself, as Microsoft software engineers are using "…AI-assisted coding" for large parts of their overall coding.

And while this sounds like a countdown to doomsday, in which all those employees are replaced by machines, which kicks off a dystopian nightmare of persistently high unemployment, this may not be the case at all. Suleyman also looks for a "major AI safety incident" to take place in the next two to three years without some kind of large-scale regulatory mechanism in place to prevent such things.

"More Flexible, Connected Future"

And for those whose jobs will be lost to AI in the near future, Microsoft has a plan waiting for you there, too. It is working to build a "…more flexible, connected future" for the Xbox platform, which it expects to reveal soon. With this year being the 25th anniversary of Xbox, it is little surprise that Microsoft has some big announcements to come.

The Game Developers Conference kicks off next month in San Francisco, and with its arrival, Microsoft will have a range of panels waiting to show off the future as it sees it. This includes one panel called "Building for the Future with Xbox," which will likely include a portion about why the next Xbox is really just a glorified PC now.

Is Microsoft a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on MSFT stock based on 32 Buys and four Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 1.61% loss in its share price over the past year, the average MSFT price target of $593.38 per share implies 46.67% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson
